name: azure-mgmt-apicenter-py
description: A Python SDK for managing Azure API Center resources including APIs, versions, specifications, deployments, and workspaces. Enables AI assistants to programmatically interact with Azure API Center for API inventory management and governance.
qualityReason: Clear Python SDK for API Center
capabilities: Create, read, update, and delete API resources in Azure API Center
Manage API versions and specifications including OpenAPI and other formats
Handle API deployments and workspace configurations
List and query APIs across multiple workspaces and collections
Work with API definitions and documentation metadata
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill providing documentation and guidance for using the Azure API Center Management SDK for Python. Static analysis scanned 0 files (0 lines) and detected 0 potential security issues. The skill contains no executable code, scripts, network calls, filesystem access, or external command invocations. Risk score is 0/100. This is a safe, documentation-focused skill for AI assistants to help users interact with Azure API Center.
valueStatement: Azure API Center provides a centralized repository for discovering and managing APIs across an organization. This skill enables AI assistants to interact with Azure API Center using the official Python SDK, allowing developers to automate API inventory management, governance, and lifecycle operations.
userTitle: Manage Azure API Center with Python SDK
limitations: Does not execute actual API calls - provides guidance and code generation only
Requires valid Azure credentials and subscription access to function
Cannot bypass Azure RBAC permissions or policies
Does not provide real-time API monitoring or analytics

useCases.targetUser: Python developers integrating Azure API Center
useCases.title: Generate SDK initialization code
useCases.description: Create Python code to authenticate and initialize the Azure API Center client for a specific subscription and resource group.

useCases.title: Automate API inventory operations
useCases.description: Generate scripts to bulk-create, update, or migrate API definitions across multiple workspaces or environments.

useCases.title: Document API governance policies
useCases.description: Create documentation and code examples for enforcing API standards, versioning strategies, and lifecycle management workflows.

promptTemplates.title: Initialize API Center client
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to authenticate and create a client for Azure API Center using the Python SDK with service principal credentials.
promptTemplates.scenario: Getting started with Azure API Center SDK

promptTemplates.title: List all APIs in workspace
promptTemplates.prompt: Write Python code to list all APIs registered in a specific workspace, including their versions and specifications.

promptTemplates.title: Create new API version
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate code to add a new API version with OpenAPI 3.0 specification to an existing API in Azure API Center.

promptTemplates.title: Manage API deployments
promptTemplates.prompt: Show how to create and manage API deployments across different environments using the Azure API Center SDK.

bestPractices: Use managed identities or service principals for production authentication rather than storing credentials in code
Implement retry policies with exponential backoff when calling Azure SDK methods to handle transient failures
Organize APIs by workspace to separate development, staging, and production API catalogs
antiPatterns: Hardcoding Azure credentials in source code or configuration files - use environment variables or Azure Key Vault instead
Ignoring API versioning - always create new versions rather than overwriting existing ones to maintain compatibility
Granting overly broad permissions to the SDK client - follow principle of least privilege for RBAC roles

faq.question: What authentication methods does this SDK support?
faq.answer: The SDK supports Azure Active Directory authentication including managed identities, service principals, and user credentials. Use DefaultAzureCredential for automatic credential resolution in Azure environments.

faq.question: Can this skill help with migrating APIs from other platforms?
faq.answer: This skill can generate code to import API definitions from OpenAPI, Swagger, or other formats into Azure API Center. However, the actual migration requires valid source API specifications and Azure API Center access.

faq.question: Does this skill support Azure API Center v2?
faq.answer: Yes, this SDK supports the latest Azure API Center service. Check the Azure SDK release notes for version-specific features and breaking changes.

faq.question: How do I handle rate limiting with the API Center SDK?
faq.answer: Implement exponential backoff retry policies. The Azure SDK does not include built-in rate limiting handling, so use libraries like tenacity or implement custom retry logic.

faq.question: Can I use this skill offline?
faq.answer: The skill provides documentation and code generation guidance. It can generate example code offline but cannot make actual Azure API calls without network connectivity.

faq.question: What Azure regions support API Center?
faq.answer: Azure API Center is available in most Azure regions. Check the Azure portal or documentation for the latest regional availability.
